title: Keraselma cyparissias
intro: Keraselma cyparissias, also known as Cypress Varnishwort or Keraselma, is a small, herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique ability to secrete a resinous sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Keraselma cyparissias include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Keraselma is often prepared as a tincture or a resin, and is used to treat a range of ailments, from coughs and colds to skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Keraselma cyparissias belongs to the family Clavicipitaceae, which includes species of fungi that are commonly known as 'hypocrealean' fungi. This classification is part of a larger group known as the Ascomycota, a division of fungi that produce ascospores. The specific classification of Keraselma cyparissias can be broken down further into the genus Keraselma and the species K. cyparissias. The family Clavicipitaceae is characterized by the presence of a unique fungus-bacterium symbiosis. Keraselma cyparissias is likely to be a parasitic or endophytic fungus that infects the cypress tree, Cyperus cyparissias.
